title: AMC Theatres Posts Higher Revenue on Attendance Rebound in Latest Quarter
text:
Movie Theater Industry Outlook and Recovery Analysis

Better Hollywood box office led AMC Entertainment Holdings to higher theater attendance and revenue in the first quarter of 2026. U.S. markets attendance was up 14 percent to 30.7 million filmgoers, against a year-earlier 27 million attendance figure. This represents a positive sign for the theater chain's recovery strategy.

The improved Q1 performance was driven by blockbuster releases and improved consumer demand for theatrical experiences. The quarter showcased the theater chain's ability to capitalize on strong film slates and convert that content into higher attendance numbers.

Despite the positive Q1 results, the broader industry continues to grapple with structural challenges. Theater attendance at AMC in 2023 approximated 240 million, up from 201 million one year before, though still significantly below the 356 million reported in 2019, before the pandemic.

Wedbush analyst Alicia Reese upgraded AMC stock from neutral to outperform, arguing that AMC is poised to benefit from a more consistent release slate over the next several quarters and gain market share through 2026 in North America and Europe. Industry observers note there is room for optimism that consumer demand persists and just needs an increased supply of better quality movies.
